title: adenostemma lavenia var. microcephalum
intro: Adenostemma lavenia var. microcephalum is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Adenostemma lavenia plant, also known as the 'blue porterweed' or 'small-headed adenostemma', which is a flowering plant native to tropical regions. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The leaves and flowers of Adenostemma lavenia var. microcephalum are rich in bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in traditional remedies for its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects.
